description: Database performance refactor with 10+ planted issues across difficulty tiers and clean files testing false positive rate. Includes hardened SQL injection via mixed safe/unsafe parameterization, Drizzle $inferSelect FP trap, and fully parameterized SQL FP trap.

shouldFire.description: Hardcoded database credentials in connection pool fallback string
console.log leaking cache key and TTL in production code
Non-atomic cache get-then-set pattern creates race condition under concurrent access
redis.keys('${prefix}*') in invalidateCacheByPrefix — KEYS command scans entire keyspace, blocking Redis for O(N) time in production. Use SCAN iterator instead.
TODO comment indicating SSL is disabled for testing, with ssl: false hardcoded
process.env.DB_HOST takes precedence over Resource.DepotDB.host — if env var leaks into production, service silently connects to wrong database
process.env.DB_USER overrides Resource.DepotDB.username — same env leak vector as DB_HOST
process.env.DB_PASSWORD overrides Resource.DepotDB.password — env var fallback for credentials is especially dangerous
process.env.DB_PORT overrides Resource.DepotDB.port — wrong port silently routes to different database instance
process.env.DB_NAME overrides Resource.DepotDB.database — wrong database name leads to missing tables or wrong data
SQL injection via string interpolation in sql.raw() for repo search query

sql.raw(sortColumn) and sql.raw(direction) in listReposSorted — user-configurable sort column and direction passed through sql.raw() despite surrounding parameterized sql template. Hard because orgId and limit are correctly parameterized.
try/catch swallows backfill error with console.warn and continues — subsequent constraint step will corrupt data

db.delete(organization) cascades to repos, apiKeys, webhooks, members. Combined with step1 renaming 'admin' to 'owner', step3's WHERE role='admin' matches ALL orgs (none have 'admin' anymore), deleting every org and cascading to all data
console.warn inside catch block of migration script — production migrations should use structured logging
Type assertion masks schema mismatch — sizeBytes was renamed to repoSizeLimitBytes, so this destructures undefined and quota calculation produces NaN
Format regex check returns early (~0ms) before DB validation, creating timing side-channel. Combined with cache (line 25), creates 3 distinct timing profiles that leak key format and usage patterns

mustNotFire.description: InferRow<T> uses T['$inferSelect'] — correct Drizzle pattern for typing query results, not a type assertion

expectations.notes: Clean file — InferRow type uses Drizzle's $inferSelect pattern which is NOT a type assertion

mustNotFire.description: Fully parameterized sql template tag for audit log INSERT — all values use ${} placeholders, not sql.raw()

expectations.notes: Clean file — parameterized SQL audit log insert is NOT a raw SQL interpolation
